sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 12

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 12:1
items.html: (to the question) For every level necessarily comprises five discernments NRNHY [Nefesh, Ruach, Neshama, Haya, Yechida], each of which must in itself also have complete NRNHY, since the degree of Ibur [conception] is not completed before there is in it the discernment of GAR of Ibur, and so on in this way — such that in the discernment of Neshama there must be twenty-five discernments of NRNHY, and likewise in the discernment of Haya, and likewise in the discernment of Yechida. And it is known that when the upper one rises for the reception of Neshama, the drop of the Zivug [coupling] of AB-SAG lowers for it the Hey Tachtona [lower Hey] from the Eynaim [eyes] to the Awzen [ear]; then it has three Kelim [vessels] and a Masach [screen] of Behina Bet [the second discernment], and it receives the level of Bina and the Light of Neshama. Therefore the GE [Galgalta ve Eynaim] of the lower one as well, which cleave to those AHP [Awzen, Hotem, Peh], are also only of the discernment of GE of Awzen, like the AHP of the upper one, and in neither of them is there anything at all of the Light of Haya, since the Hey Tachtona has not yet descended from the Eynaim to the Hotem [nose], which returns the AHP of the discernment of Hochma. Therefore nothing can be included in that Rucha Kadma'a [first spirit] except of the degree of Neshama. And so, after the lower one has already received from there all the degrees it has to receive of the discernment of Neshama, the "manner of women" ceases for the upper one, for it has nothing more to give it. Therefore, when the upper one rises to receive Mochin of Haya, that entire order explained above for the Mochin of Neshama then begins anew. For when it receives the drop of Zivug that lowers the Hey Tachtona from the Awzen to the Hotem — whereupon the upper one raises its AHP of the discernment of Hochma, in which is a Masach of Behina Gimel [the third discernment] — it necessarily raises together with them also the Galgalta ve Eynaim of the lower one that cleave to those AHP of the discernment of Haya. They too are thereby included in the Zivug, and upon their added MAN [Mayin Nukvin] there emerges once again a discernment of that Rucha Kadma'a, of the discernment of Haya — and likewise all the discernments named above: Bi'a Kadma'a [first coming], Rucha Kadma'a, Kli Kadma'a [first vessel], first MAN in the discernment of firstborn sons, the matter of the double portion the firstborn takes, the matter of the Nesira [sawing apart], the equal level at the back, the Zivug Panim be Panim [face-to-face], and so forth, until the lower one takes all its discernments from that Rucha of Haya as well — and then the "manner of women" ceases once more. And in this same way all the above discernments repeat again with the upper one's attainment of the Mochin of Yechida. Thus, for every new discernment not included in that Rucha Kadma'a, all the discernments that were at first, in the Mochin of Neshama, repeat again. (Inner Observation, Item 4)
